notes.type_correctness: CommandGrid.tsx is fully typed with no implicit any. Two pre-existing typecheck failures were found and fixed: (1) server/config.test.ts used ReturnType<typeof vi.spyOn> which conflicts with overloaded fs function signatures — fixed by typing spy variables as `any`; (2) client/tsconfig.json did not exclude test files, causing CommandGrid.test.tsx to be compiled during `cd client && npm run build` — fixed by adding exclude patterns.
notes.pattern_adherence: getApiBase() used correctly for API calls. No module-level caches. Kebab-case server files, PascalCase React component. Section ordering uses module-level constants consistent with project conventions.
notes.test_coverage: 53 pure-logic tests in server/command-grid-logic.test.ts cover all ordering, filtering, display-name, tooltip, and edge-case scenarios. CommandGrid.test.tsx is a future-infra file (requires @testing-library/react + jsdom) and is excluded from the current test run — coverage for DOM rendering is deferred. The test file itself documents this clearly.
notes.security: No new attack surface. The `spawnCommand` function sends cmd.slug to the API — slugs are string identifiers, not user-supplied input. No hardcoded paths, no SQL, no secrets.
notes.architectural_alignment: Pure-logic tests correctly placed in server/ to run under existing Node/vitest configuration rather than requiring new client-side test infra. CommandGrid.test.tsx placed in client/src/ for future infra but excluded from build via tsconfig. Follows hub-mode patterns throughout.

flags.message: client/src/components/CommandGrid.test.tsx exists but cannot run — requires @testing-library/react, jsdom, and a client vitest config. DOM rendering is unverified by automated tests today.
flags.recommendation: Add @testing-library/react + jsdom + vitest client config as a follow-up to enable the full component test suite.

flags.message: Client bundle is 1,083 kB (pre-existing warning, not introduced by this feature). CommandGrid adds no new large dependencies — only lucide-react icons already in use.
